title: mirabilis jalapa var. ambigua
intro: Mirabilis jalapa var. ambigua, also known as the rose mallow or Mexican four o'clock, is a variety of the Mirabilis jalapa plant that is native to the Americas and is commonly found in wetlands and along streams. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, with its roots and leaves possessing medicinal properties that include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Mirabilis jalapa var. ambigua include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, and it can also be used topically to promote wound healing and reduce pain.
